BRIEF ROPE - NO DAMAGE REPORTED
CARPORT BLOWN INTO POWERLINES DUE TO STRONG WINDS.

PHOTOS AND VIDEO VIA SOCIAL MEDIA SHOWED THREE, BRIEF LANDSPOUTS TO THE SOUTH OF SMYER. THESE LANDSPOUTS FORMED AS A PAIR OF OUTFLOW BOUNDARIES COLLIDED ACROSS SOUTHEAST HOCKLEY COUNTY. THIS RESULTED IN THE FORMATION OF PULSATING, MULTI-CELLULAR T-STORM CLUSTERS THAT TEMPORARILY REMAINED ROOTED ALONG THESE OUTFLOWS, CAUSING A SPIKE IN NEAR-SURFACE VORTICITY BEFORE QUICKLY DISSIPATING AS THE BOUNDARIES SURGED AWAY FROM THE T-STORM UPDRAFTS.
WEST TEXAS MESONET
WEST TEXAS MESONET

DELAYED REPORT OF A FEW DOWNED POWER POLES AND A SNAPPED FENCE POST. ALSO STATED THAT A PATCH OF GRASS AND SUNFLOWERS WERE BENT IN AN EAST TO NORTHEASTERLY DIRECTION. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
LANDSPOUT TORNADO OBSERVED BENEATH A CONVECTIVE TOWER ALONG AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVING TO THE NORTHWEST. THE LANDSPOUT WAS VISIBLE FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
A SECOND LANDSPOUT TORNADO WAS OBSERVED FARTHER WEST ALONG THE SAME OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVING NORTHWEST THROUGH SOUTHERN LUBBOCK COUNTY. THIS LANDSPOUT WAS ALSO VISIBLE FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
STRONG T-STORM OUTFLOW WINDS DESTROYED A CARPORT, BLEW THE DOORS OFF A BARN, AND BROKE OFF TREE LIMBS IN KIRKLAND.
